ae55a88a7.title: The Attribution Impossibility: No Feature Ranking Is Faithful, Stable, and Complete Under Collinearity
ae55a88a7.body:
arXiv:2605.21492v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: No feature ranking can be simultaneously faithful, stable, and complete when features are collinear. For collinear pairs, ranking reduces to a coin flip. 点击下方「查看原文」阅读完整内容。

ad01feb8a.title: Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning
ad01feb8a.body:
Reward hacking occurs when a reinforcement learning (RL) agent exploits flaws or ambiguities in the reward function to achieve high rewards, without genuinely learning or completing the intended task. Reward hacking exists because RL environments are often imperfect, and it is fundamentally challenging to accurately specify a reward function.
